title: Elevate UI Aesthetics: Master CSS 'text-underline-offset' & 'text-decoration-thickness'
tweet: Customizing underlines beyond the default improves UI readability & aesthetics. Master CSS 'text-underline-offset' & 'text-decoration-thickness' for beautifully styled links & emphasized text. #CSSTypography #UIDesign #WebFonts #UX
tip: Traditional browser underlines often cut through descenders or appear too thin/thick, detracting from readability and visual appeal. Use 'text-underline-offset' to position the underline away from the text baseline and 'text-decoration-thickness' to control its stroke width. This allows for precise, design-aligned underlines that enhance legibility and create a polished user interface. Apply these properties to links, highlighted text, or even custom decorative elements.
css_snippet: a { text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-color: var(--primary-color); text-decoration-thickness: 0.08em; /* Adjust for desired weight */ text-underline-offset: 0.2em; /* Adjust for desired offset */ /* For older browsers, consider a bottom border fallback */ /* border-bottom: 2px solid var(--primary-color); */ }
